items.headline: Quiet Weekend After a Pullback; Asia Tech Sell-off Noted
items.summary: U.S. markets concluded the week on a subdued note, with major indices closing lower on Friday, June 27, 2026, breaking a two-week winning streak for the S&P 500. Global sentiment over the weekend has been impacted by a significant tech-led sell-off in Asian markets, notably the KOSPI plunging nearly 6% and the Nikkei falling 4.15% on Saturday, June 28, 2026. Investors appear to be rotating towards more defensive sectors like healthcare.

items.statContext: U.S. stock markets are closed today, June 28, 2026, for the weekend.

items.headline: Eli Lilly Surges Amid Defensive Rotation
items.summary: Eli Lilly and Company saw significant gains as investors sought defensive growth amidst cooling tech momentum.

items.headline: Moderna Leads S&P 500 Gainers
items.summary: Moderna was among the top S&P 500 gainers on Friday, June 26, 2026.

items.headline: ServiceNow Records Notable Uptick
items.summary: ServiceNow showed strong performance, closing up nearly 10% on Friday.

items.headline: Datadog Sees Strong Performance
items.summary: Datadog experienced a solid gain at the close of trading on Friday.

items.headline: Tech and Industrials Lagged
items.summary: Specific top individual stock losers for the U.S. market from Friday, June 26, 2026, were not readily available; however, industrials and technology sectors led the laggards on Friday. The broader market saw a pullback in technology shares due to AI concerns and interest rate worries.

items.headline: Healthcare Sector Shows Strength
items.summary: Healthcare stocks demonstrated robust performance last week, rising 7.5% on average, as investors sought bargains and defensive growth opportunities. The sector was a leading performer on June 26, 2026.

items.headline: Technology Sector Under Pressure Amid AI Jitters
items.summary: The technology sector experienced a significant pullback, dropping 5 ranks to #9 on June 26, 2026, due to renewed questions around AI spending, interest rates, and valuations. Chinese hedge funds have warned that the rapid rise in artificial intelligence stocks may be nearing a 'super bubble' breaking point. This sentiment was echoed in Asian markets with a violent tech-led sell-off on Saturday.

items.headline: Energy and Commodities Under Pressure
items.summary: The energy and commodities sectors faced significant pressure last week, with oil prices selling off hard, down over 25% in the past month. Oil (CM:CL) fell 3.75% to $70.07 on Friday as supply fears eased after traffic through the Strait of Hormuz resumed. The Oil & Gas sector led declines in some markets, falling 4.66%.

items.headline: Industrials Show Stability
items.summary: The Industrials sector held a stable leading position on June 26, 2026.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Maintained Rates, Hawkish Stance on Inflation
items.summary: The Federal Reserve held its target range for the federal funds rate steady at 3-1/2 to 3-3/4 percent at its June 16-17, 2026 meeting. The committee's statement maintained a hawkish tone, emphasizing elevated inflation relative to its 2 percent goal. Minneapolis Federal Reserve President Neel Kashkari indicated that one rate hike could still occur in 2026, citing sticky inflation related to energy prices and AI infrastructure spending.

items.headline: Economic Data Releases Across Europe on June 28, 2026
items.summary: Several economic events are scheduled for Sunday, June 28, 2026, primarily across European countries. These include releases on Manufacturing Confidence in the Netherlands, Consumer Confidence in Finland and Portugal, Industrial Output in Lithuania, Trade Balance in Sweden, HICP Flash YY in Spain, Retail Sales in Spain, Sweden, and Ireland, Money-M3 in the EU, Mortgage Lending and Approvals in Great Britain, BOE Consumer Credit in Great Britain, Business Confidence in Portugal and the EU, Economic Sentiment in the EU, and CPI in Belgium.

items.headline: Micron Technology Reports Record Q3 Results
items.summary: Micron Technology ($MU) announced record results for its third quarter of fiscal 2026, which ended May 28, 2026. The company reported revenue of $41.46 billion and GAAP net income of $28.24 billion. Major tech earnings reports expected in late July include Alphabet ($GOOGL) on July 28, 2026, Meta Platforms ($META) on July 29, 2026, and Apple ($AAPL) on July 30, 2026.

items.headline: Bitcoin Spot ETFs See Record Outflows
items.summary: Bitcoin spot ETFs experienced $1.79 billion in net outflows last week, marking the second-largest weekly redemption on record. Bitcoin's price hovered around the $60,000 level, briefly sliding to $58,126 before a slight recovery.
